def: s.m. Baptism (see baptismā).
s.m. The Christian rite of baptism. (The term has been introduced into the Indian dialects by the Christian missionary).
s.m. The corporeal frame, the body.
adj. (f. -ī), Helpless, distressed, wretched ( = bāpṛā, q.v.).
adj. Poor, helpless, destitute.
s.m. The corporeal frame, the body.
s.f. = bāṛ, q.v.
s.m. = bāl, 'hair' q.v.; — also = bāl, 'child'; and bālā, 'girl,' q.q.v.

s.f. Time, turn; occasion, opportunity; delay; (and, in Hindī) s.m. Day of the week; Saturday; — prohibition, obstacle, obstruction, &c.; (in Hindī and Urdū), gate, door, door-way, threshold, entrance; admission, admittance; permission; (in Urdū and Persian), sitting of a sovereign, &c. to give audience, court or levée (cf. dar-bār), tribunal, assembly, convention, congress; — a suffix forming Persian 'nouns of multitude,' e.g. sang-bār, joʼe-bār, q.q.v.
subs: adv. Time after time, again and again, repeatedly; constantly, every moment; several times, often ( = kaʼī bār); — bār-bār-karke, adv. After several times or turns
s.m. Private court or hall of audience, privy-council chamber
(-ko), To give admission, to admit; to give permission
s.f. lit. 'Stoppage at the gate'; money exacted by the bridegroom's sister before allowing the bride and bridegroom to enter the latter's house
s.f. Place of audience, court; palace
v.n. To delay, to hesitate; to place obstacles in the way; to raise objections
s.f. Admission, admittance, access; audience; — bār-yābī-ě-darbār, s.f. Admission to a court or levée.

def: s.m. Load, burden; cargo; weight, heaviness; onus; pregnancy; fruit, produce; (in Persian phrases), greatness, dignity; God; — adj. Heavy, burdensome, hard to be borne
subs: s.f. Division of the crops by sheaves or shocks before the corn is trodden out

adj. & s.m. Burden-bearing; burden-bearer, porter, carrier, beast of burden; cart; — bār-bardārī, s.f. Means of conveyance, carriage, transport; cost of conveyance, cart-hire, camel-hire, &c.; freight, transit charges; lading, cargo; — bār-bardārī-ke jānwar, s.m. Beasts of burden; draught cattle; cattle
s.m. Whoremongery; — bār-bilāsinī, s.f. Harlot ( = bār-strī); — bār-bilāsī, s.m. Whoremonger
s.m. The burden of disproof or rebutment
s.m. Load or trouble of mind; tiresomeness
bār-ḵẖudā or ḵẖudāyā, intj. Lord God! Great God ( = bār-ě-ilāhā)

adj. Loaded; laden with fruit; fruitful; pregnant; bār-dārī, s.f. Fruitfulness: = bār-dāna, s.m. Supplies, provisions; bags, &c. in which provisions are kept; vessels, utensils; implements or apparatus of a trade
(-ko), To load; to impose, give trouble, encumber
s.m. Beast of burden (as an ox, elephant, &c.); burden-bearer, porter, carrier; cart, truck, &c. ( = bhār-kas)

ety: of bārīdan = Pehl. vārānītan; Zend vār; S. वार्
def: adj. Scattering pearls).
s.m. Water.
s.f. Volley (of mus.ketry, &c.)
subs: bāṛ jhāṛnā, v.n. To fire a volley.

def: s.f. Increase, &c. = bāṛh, q.v.
s.f. Mixture of syrup and rice-flour, of which the sweetmeat called andarsā is made.

s.m. Choosing, selecting; choice, election, wish, request, boon, favour, blessing, good; anything chosen as a present; gift, reward, recompense; one who chooses, chooser; one who solicits a girl in marriage, suitor, wooer, lover; bridegroom, son-in-law; husband, lord, master; — breadth (of cloth); — adj. Best, excellent, precious
subs: (S. vara+varṇa+ka), adj. Of the best colour, of an excellent colour; white, fair
s.m. Granting a boon; benefaction; wedding-gift to a bride from her betrothed; answer to a prayer addressed to God or a saint
bar dāyak, adj. & s.m. Granting or vouchsafing a blessing; — giver of a choice; granter of a boon, vouchsafer of a blessing

def: conj. But, moreover, even, &c. ( = baran and par, q.q.v.).
s.f. Asparagus racemosus.
s.m. The Indian fig-tree, Ficus indica ( = baṛ).
s.m. Breast, bosom, chest; — ( = S. uru or vara), breadth (of cloth); — fruit ( = phal)
subs: adj. & s.f. = bar-ḵẖẉurd, and bar-ḵẖẉurdār, q.q.v.

s.f. lit. 'Eating or enjoying the fruit'; success, prosperity; enjoyment, happiness, the obtaining one's desires; — bar-ḵẖẉurdār, adj. & s.m. Prosperous, successful, happy, enjoying long life and prosperity; blessed with a family of sons; — male issue, son, child; — bar-ḵẖẉurdārī, s.f. Happiness, &c. ( = barḵẖẉurd); numerous issue or offspring
s.m. lit. 'Chest-swelling'; inflammation of the diaphragm, pleurisy
bar hāṅknā, v.n. To boast, brag.

ety: act. part. of burdan, rt. Zend bar = S.भर्
def: adj. & s.m. Bearing, carrying, taking, seizing, taking away, carrying off; — bearer, carrier, &c. (used in comp., e.g. dil-bar, adj. & s.m. ravishing, or ravisher of, hearts; — nāma-bar, s.m. bearer of a letter.)
s.m. The banyan or Indian fig-tree, Ficus indica
subs: baṛ-kaulā, s.m. Fruit of the banyan tree.

def: s.f. Wasp (barr, barerā, bhir).

ety: prop. bāṛh, S. वृति, rt. वृ cf. S. वाट
def: s.f. Fence, hedge, outer boundary or enclosure (of any kind); line, border, rim, margin, edge; edge (of a weapon or tool = S. पालि); line or row (of soldiers); front, frout rank; — (in comp.), A suffix signifying 'place,' enclosure, &c. ( = wār)
subs: bāṛ bāṅdhnā (meṅ), To fence, enclose, hedge in
bāṛ-par ćaṛhānā (-ko), To encourage, instigate, incite, excite
(-par), bāṛ ćīrnā, bāṛ denā, bāṛ dilwānā, bāṛ rakhnā (-par), To put an edge (on), give an edge (to), to sharpen, whet
v.t. To blunt or notch the edge (of a sword, &c.)

def: s.m. Perquisite of the ahīr or cowherd in milk (generally the milk of every eighth day).
adj. Great, large, excessive, &c.; (much used in comp. e.g.), baṛ-bolā, s.m. Noisy, talkative person; boaster, braggart
subs: adj. & s.m. Very fortunate; — one who is very fortunate
adj. & s.m. Big-bellied, pot-bellied; greedy, gluttonous; avaricious; — one who has a big belly; a greedy person, glutton
adj. Having large teeth; (in Bot.) sinuate
adj. & s.m. Having long ears; — long-eared man
adj. & s.m. Having a large nose; — a man with a big nose
adj. & s.m. Large-lipped, thick-lipped; — thick-lipped man.

def: s.m. The process of wire-drawing (through the jantrī).
adj. Twelve ( = bārah, q.v.)
subs: s.f. lit. 'Twelve-fold force (zor)'; force, violence, compulsion; — adv. By force; — bārā-jorī karnā, To use force, bear strongly upon, to press, constrain, compel

s.f. (in Gram.), The aggregate of the twelve short vowels of the Nāgarī alphabet in combination with consonants (e.g. ka, kā, ki, kī, &c.); a kind of song, every line of which begins with a consonant.

def: s.m. Enclosed space, enclosure ( = gherā), area; cemetery; — fence, hedge ( = bāṛ); alms, charity (in this sense prob. connected with varaṇam)
adj. (f. -ī), Large, great, big, vast, immense, huge; grand, noble, high, exalted, eminent; grown up, old, senior, elder; superior, supreme, principal; grave, serious; vital, essential, important
subs: s.m. Uncle; father-in-law; (slang) an astute fellow, consummate knave
s.m. Great or rich man; man of eminence or rank, grandee
s.m. Superior officer, head of a department
s.m. (Math.) The greatest common measure (syn. maqsūm-ilaih ʻāz̤am)
s.m. A kind of appraisement; money borrowed at a very high interest
s.m. High rank or station; dignity, consequence, importance, distinction: baṛā din, s.m. Great, or long, day; Christmas day
v.n. lit. 'To take the long journey or road'; to die
s.m. Great man; chief civil functionary; a resident (at court)
s.m. Large workshop, or establishment; extensive business; — baṛā karnā or kar-denā, v.t. To enlarge, increase, augment, lengthen; to exalt, promote; to extinguish (a light = baṛhānā)
s.m. Large house or building; grand house; great, rich, or noble house or family
s.m. Great name or reputation; — baṛā nām karnā, v.n. To acquire a great name, win a great reputation, become famous
(obl. pl. of baṛā), the great; grown up people, &c. (see baṛe); — baṛoṅ-meṅ milnā, v.n. To be grown up; to associate with elders or seniors, or with the great
bare-buṛhe, baṛe-log, s.m. pl. Grown-up people, elders, seniors, superiors; the great, the eminent; forefathers, ancestors
s.m. Great man, person of high position or rank; — s.f. High appointment; high position
s.f. Great matter, important thing or affair; difficult affair; — baṛī bāt huʼī, A great thing has been accomplished; — intj. thank Heaven!
s.f. Elder son's wife; — a wife who is older than her husband
s.f. A form of address to an elderly or respectable woman
s.m. Old man; — master of the house; — intj. Aged sir! reverend sir!

def: s.m. Condiment made of ground pulse (dāl, mūng, or māsh), formed into lumps and fried in ghī, butter, or oil (cf. baṛī).
s.m. Drawing water for irrigation; the song sung whilst drawing water; the man who stands over the well to tilt the leathern bucket (moṭ or ćaraṣ) when it is drawn up.
= bālā, q.v.
s.m. Perquisite of the ahīr or cowherd in milk (generally the milk of every eighth day).
s.m. Time, turn; regard, respect
subs: (-ke), In respect of, concerning (e.g. kis-ke bāre-meṅ, In respect of whom?).

def: = بڙا baṛā, q.v.
s.m. Lamb; kid; fawn; the sign Aries.
s.m. Wind, &c. = bayār, q.v.

ety: for A. برآءة, v.n. fr. برٲ, cf. barī
def: s.f. A writing conferring immunity or exemption; commission, warrant, decree, assignment, letter; draught, cheque; — the fourteenth day of the month Shaʻbān, &c. (see shab-ě-barāt).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi برات (brat /⁠barāt⁠/) (c. 1693), from Sanskrit वरयात्रा (varayātrā), a compound of वर (vará, “suitor, bridegroom, husband”) + यात्रा (yā́trā, “journey”), literally “the groom's journey [to the bride]”.

def: adv. = bār-bār, and bārambār, q.q.v.

s.m. Equal, peer, compeer; — adv. Regularly, uniformly, invariably, constantly; precisely, exactly; approximately; along with, together, side by side; conjointly, concurrently; straight on, continuously, without break or intermission, directly, immediately, without delay
subs: (-ke), To come on a level (with), come up (to), to overtake
adv. (emphat. of barābar), Breast to breast, shoulder to shoulder, side by side; in a row or line
adj. (f. -ī), Of the same size, weight, age, or standing, &c.; equal

v.t. To make level, plane, or smooth; to level, raze, demolish, destroy; to efface, rub or wipe out; to run through, waste, spend (as a fortune); to bring to a termination, complete, finish (a work); to liquidate or discharge (a debt); to make equal, like, or similar, to equalize, assimilate, balance, accord, match, adapt, compose, adjust

barābar-kī ćoṭ, s.f. Person of equal standing, rank, wealth or power; equal, peer; suitable match, partner or mate; equal shareholders; (in Mech.), equal forces acting in opposite directions; — barābar-kī ṭakkar-kā, adj. Of equal standing, rank, &c.; equally good, on a par, equal

v.n. To be level, even, smooth; to be levelled, razed, demolished; to be effaced, erased, wiped out; to be equal (to), on a par (with); to suit, fit, match, agree, coincide (with), be enough (for); to balance; to come abreast (of), come up (to), overtake ( = barābar ānā); to keep pace (with), keep up (with); to be complete; to be full, be up to the brim (as a hole); to terminate, come to an end, be expended, spent, run through, wasted; to be settled, adjusted, or discharged (as an account or debt); to be even (with), be quits.

def: s.f. Level, par, equality, equalization, equilibrium, equipoise, balance; exactness, agreement, correspondence; parallelism; competition, emulation, rivalry; presumption, insolence
subs: (-kī), To equal; ṭo try to equal; to copy, imitate; to vie (with), emulate, rival; to cope or strive (with); to confront, withstand, oppose, resist, defy; to be insolent or presumptuous.

def: s.m. Bull, ox, bullock ( = balad).
adj. Conferring a boon; granting a request or prayer; favourable, propitious (see bar, 'boon').

ety: old P. burādar; Pehl. brāt; Zend brātar; S. भ्रातर
def: s.m. Brother; relation, relative, kinsman
subs: s.m. Step-brother; uterine brother (syn. gailaṛ bhāʼī)
adj. Kind to relations, or to brethren and friends
s.m. Own brother, full brother (syn. sagā bhāʼī)
s.f. Calling oneself a brother, profession of fraternal affection

def: adj. & adv. Brotherly, fraternal; like a brother, becoming a brother.
s.f. Brotherhood, fraternity, connection, relationship (syn. bhāʼī-ćārā); relation, relative, kindred, kinsfolk; community, society; band of musicians (called tāshe-wāle); — adj. Brotherly, fraternal
subs: s.m. One who is put out of caste, outcast
v.t. To expel from a fraternity; to put out of caste; to excommunicate.

def: v.t. To cover or bull (a cow); — v.n. To be bulled (a cow).
adv. For the sake of, for ( = liye); — on account of, because of, by reason of; for the purpose of, in order to
subs: adj. & adv. Nominal, ostensible, pretended, fictitious; — nominally, ostensibly, for the mere name of, in pretence or show.

def: adj. Lighted up, kindled, set on fire, inflamed.
adv. With intense desire; with avidity, eagerly.

s.m. Hog, boar; the third or boar-incarnation of Vishṇu (in which he raised the earth from the bottom of the sea with his tusks); the earth or land so raised; land next to a village (see barāh and varāh).
adj. Twelve; — bārahoṅ, coll. n. The whole twelve, the twelve
subs: adj. lit. 'Twelve roads'; scattered, dispersed, cast to the winds, ruined, destroyed; embarrassed, bewildered, perplexed, distracted, confounded; at sixes and sevens, in confusion; at variance, in a state of discord; — bārah-bāṭ karnā, v.t. To scatter, disperse, throw into confusion, confound; to ruin, destroy; — bārah bāṭ honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be scattered, &c., to become houseless, ruined, &c., to be a vagabond; to be abandoned
adj. & s.m. Generally praised or approved, pure, perfect, genuine, good; sound, hale, hearty, well, convalescent; — the most refined, the purest (gold, water, &c.)
s.m. The twelve pillars which mark the boundary of a cantonment or encampment; the cantonment or encampment so bounded
adj. & s.m. Having twelve arches; — a bridge with twelve arches
bārah-dwārī, s.f. lit. 'Having twelve doors'; a summerhouse (generally in a garden)
s.m. lit. 'Having twelve tines'; a stag, antelope (?)

adj. & s.m. Pertaining to the twelve months of the year; — Hindī verse of twelve stanzas, corresponding to the twelve months of the year, and descriptive of the pain of separation from a husband, as also of the characteristic changes of the season, the scenery, and the pastimes which distinguish each month
adv. The whole twelve months, all the year round, at all times, always, perpetually
s.f. The twelve days of Moham mad's fatal illness.

def: s.m. Boar, &c. = bārāh, q.v.
s.m. Land next to, or surrounding, a village ( = bārāh, q.v.).
adv. In or on the way; — s.m. Provisions for a journey.
adj. Twelfth; the twelfth; — bārahweṅ, bārahwe, adv. Twelfthly, in the twelfth place.
adj. & s.f. Relating to a boar, or to the third incarnation; — land next to a village ( = bārāt); a sow.
adj. & s.f. = bārāhī, q.v.
s.f. Name of the Śakti of Vishṇu in the form of a boar (varāha), the goddess of eruptive diseases; — a small kind of sugar-cane
subs: s.m. Fair held in honour of the goddess Barāhī.

def: s.m. (prov.) = baṛhaʼī, q.v.
adj. Naked, bare
s.f. Nakedness, bareness.
adj. Twelfth; the twelfth; — bārahweṅ, bārahwe, adv. Twelfthly, in the twelfth place.
s.f. = baṛāʼī; and s.m. = baṛhaʼī; q.q.v.

s.f. Largeness, greatness, bigness, vastness, bulkiness, bulk, size, magnitude, volume, extent; adult state, old age, senility; seniority, priority, precedence; rank, grandeur, dignity; respect, reverence, honour; excellence, superiority, exaltation; praise, laudation, magnifying; self-praise, boasting
subs: (-kī), To extol, praise, boast (of), &c. ( = baṛāʼī karnā); to boast of superiority (over, -par)
(-ko), To show respect (to), pay honour (to), to honour
(-kī), baṛāʼī mārnā (-kī), To magnify, praise, laud, extol; to magnify or praise oneself, to boast, vaunt.

def: s.m. (prov.) = baṛhaʼī, q.v.
s.m. Seller of betel-leaf; cultivator of betel.
s.f. The wife of a seller of betel leaf or cultivator of betel; a woman who sells betel leaf.
s.f. The wife of a seller of betel leaf or cultivator of betel; a woman who sells betel leaf.
May God prosper or bless you (a phrase expressive of approbation).
s.f. Blessings; prosperity; auspiciousness

s.f. Increase, abundance, prosperity, blessing; good fortune, auspiciousness; inherent prosperity which produces success or abundance (the word is also commonly used in weighing grain, &c. in place of the number 'one')
subs: To grant a blessing, to give increase or abundance; to bless

v.n. To be prosperous, to be complete or full; (and conversely) to have nothing to give save a blessing (e.g. when a beggar solicits alms, and is answered by barkat hai, 'there is nothing for you'; 'you have a blessing').
ex: اَچّھے کاموں سے بَرْکَتیں مِلْتی ہَیں۔
acche kāmõ se barkatẽ miltī ha͠i.
You receive blessings from doing good deeds.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برکت (barakat), from Arabic بَرَكَة (baraka).

def: v.n. = barasnā, q.v.
adv. & postp. On the contrary, contrariwise; the contrary (of, -ke); in opposition (to), in spite (of); conversely.
s.m. = baṛhal, q.v.

s.f. Coming up, coming or going out or forth, egress, outgoing, exit, issue, eruption; outgoings, drawings, expenditure, expenses; land thrown up (by a river), alluvial deposit; — informing (particularly of bribery), accusation; — perf. part. Come up, come out ( = bar-āmada)
subs: (-se), To come up, ascend, appear, rise, spring up, come forth; to come out (of or from); to accrue; to be recovered (as stolen property).

def: part. Come up, come out or forth; recovered (as stolen property); — s.m. Verandah, balcony, porch, gallery, piazza, eaves (syn. baranḍā; bārjā); outgoings, expenditure, disbursement ( = bar-āmad, q.v.).
s.m. Fruit of mango trees; revenue derived from mango groves.
adv. Time after time, again and again, repeatedly ( = bār-bār, q.v.).
adv. = بارمبار बारम्बार bārambār, q.v.

ety: of bārīdan, 'to rain,' Pehl. vārān; Zend vāra; S. vār
def: s.m. Rain; rainy season.
s.m. Forbidding, prohibiting, preventing, warding off, guarding; resistance, opposition, prohibition, defence, protection; armour, mail; an elephant.
conj. Moreover, but, even, but even, and even; whereas; nay, rather, on the contrary.
s.m. Choosing, selecting; choice; promise of marriage; surrounding, screening, covering, protecting, nourishing; enclosure, rampart, mound, causeway
subs: v.t. To hire a priest for the performance of a sacrifice or any religious ceremony.

def: s.f. Alluvial deposit or soil; fresh earth carried by water into hollows.

ety: S. वरणीयं, rt. वृ; but cf. next
def: v.n. To remain at a distance (from), hold (oneself) aloof (from), to avoid, shun; to abstain.
v.n. To be successful, to prosper; to succeed (with, -ke sāth); to prevail (against), overcome.
v.t. To drive or thrust in ( = bāṛnā; ghuseṛnā).
adj. & s.m. Raining; — that which rains or showers.
s.m. Verandah, &c. = baranḍā, q.v.
s.m. Verandah, portico, porch, piazza (syn. bar-āmda).

r: baranda, barinda, burdan, bar, añt, ant
ety: act. part. of burdan; and = bar, q.v.+Zend añt = S. ant
def: adj. & s.m. Bearing, carrying; bearer, carrier
subs: s.m. Secret emissary or agent; smuggler (syn. ćaukī-mār).

def: s.f. Rain, raining.
part. Thrown down, overthrown, upset, demolished, destroyed.
part. adj. Throwing down, overthrowing, overturning, demolishing, destroying.
s.m. Allowance of provisions; — bell; key; bar, bolt.
adv. In kind; after the manner or fashion (of), like.
s.m. = barangā; bargā, q.q.v.
s.m. Plank used for covering in the roof of a house; rafter.
part. Roused, awakened, stirred up, excited, incited; — bar-angeḵẖta karnā, v.t. To rouse, stir up, raise, incite, excite, &c. (syn. uksānā).
adj. Depending or rain; — s.f. Land depending on rain, unirrigated land (in contradistinction to ćāhī, q.v.); a woollen coat or cloak to protect one from the rain, overcoat, water-proof
subs: s.m. A field the irrigation of which is dependent on rain.

def: s.m. = baṛhāʼo, q.v.
s.m. Avoiding, shunning, avoidance (of, -se); being cautious (of), being on one's guard (against); abstaining (from); abstinence (syn. baćāʼo
subs: (-se), To avoid, shun, be cautious (of), be on one's guard (against); to abstain (from).

def: s.m. Largeness, greatness, bulk, &c. ( = baṛāʼī, q.v.); grandeur, dignity, exaltation, elevation.
s.m. Largeness, greatness, bulk, &c. ( = baṛāʼī, q.v.); grandeur, dignity, exaltation, elevation.

ety: bar, 'up or out'+ār, act. part. of āwardan, 'to bring'
def: adj. & s.m. Bringing or bearing up, bringing to pass; — that which brings up or to pass; raiser up, bearer up, taker, bearer; one who, or that which, effects or accomplishes (an object), accomplisher, performer, producer; — effecting, accomplishment; — produce of taxation; taxation, tax (in general)
s.f. Drove of bullocks laden with grain, betel, cotton, &c.
s.m. Thong, rope, string (especially of a swing; cf. barrā and barhā).
s.m. A shareholder paying his portion of the jamaʻ according to the barār taxation.
s.m. Year; — raining, rain
subs: s.m. A Hindū marriage ceremony (the taking away from the bride at the close of the first year after marriage the god or bundle of cocoanut, rice, &c. given to her at the time of marriage)
baras-din, baras-roz, s.m. The whole year; — adv. All the year round
s.f. A cow or buffalo that calves every year
(or roz-) -kā, adj. A year's; — baras-din-kā din, s.m. An annual festival or holiday
(S. varsha+granthi), s.f. The ceremony of tying a knot on the anniversary of one's birthday; (hence) anniversary of a birthday.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi बरस (barasa), from Prakrit 𑀯𑀭𑀺𑀲 (varisa), semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वर्ष (varṣá).

def: s.m. (f.?), An intoxicating drug made of opium.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi बरस (barasa), from Prakrit 𑀯𑀭𑀺𑀲 (varisa), semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वर्ष (varṣá).

def: s.m. Leprosy (particularly the malignant white species thereof termed 'leuce': syn. apras).
s.f. See s.v. baras, 'year.'
adj. Excited, distracted ( = āshufta, q.v.).

v.n. To rain, be wet; to fall like rain, fall in showers, be poured or showered down; to be showered, shed, scattered; to shed or cast forth beams (of light), to sparkle, glow; to burst, discharge (as a boil); to be winnowed, be sifted (as grain)
subs: (-par), A torrent (of invective, &c.) to be poured out (on).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀭𑀺𑀲𑀇 (varisaï), from Sanskrit वर्षति (varṣati).

def: s.m. Raining; rain
subs: adj. Raining; rainy; about to rain, threatening rain ( = barsāʼū, q.v.).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀭𑀺𑀲𑀇 (varisaï), from Sanskrit वर्षति (varṣati).

def: s.m. Rain ( = barasnā).
adj. (f. -wīṅ), Yearly, annual, anniversary
s.f. Marriage procession = barāt, q.v.
s.f. Thong, leathern girth; rope, cable; — mark, streak, weal.
s.m. A disease affecting rice-crops.
adj. Burning, blazing, flaming (see barnā, imperf. part. bartā).

s.m. Any religious act or obligation enjoined by the gods; a self-chosen or voluntary act, any meritorious act of devotion or austerity, a devout observance, fasting, continence, penance, vow, &c. (syn. upās; but this is more correctly applied to a compulsory abstention from food, as through inability to obtain it, or through sickness; whereas brat or barat has a strictly religious signification).
s.f. The procession of the bridegroom (to the house of the bride), marriage procession; the company or attendants at a marriage; multitude, flock, assemblage, troop, crowd; — a suit at cards
subs: The setting out of the bridegroom in procession (to the house of the bride)
barāt meṅ jānā, v.n. To join in a marriage procession.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi برات (brat /⁠barāt⁠/) (c. 1693), from Sanskrit वरयात्रा (varayātrā), a compound of वर (vará, “suitor, bridegroom, husband”) + यात्रा (yā́trā, “journey”), literally “the groom's journey [to the bride]”.

def: s.f. (?) Land situated in the midst of jungles.
s.m. = bareṭhā, q.v.
s.m. One who joins a marriage procession, or attends a marriage.
s.f. Orthography.
s.m. Cotton land (cf. badar).
s.m. Part of the wall above the door or entrance; keystone (of an arch, &c.).
s.m. = bādav, q.v.
s.f. A certain bird that feeds on fish.
adj. = bārah-wāṅ, q.v.
s.f. Calculating, estimating, considering beforehand what may be necessary for a feast, &c.; calculation, estimate, budget; pay-abstract
subs: v.t. To strike out (of a muster-roll); to calculate, estimate; to carry forward or to account.

def: part. Brought or carried forward, carried to account; — s.m. Estimate, &c. = bar-āward.
s.m.f. Creatures; people; nobles.
s.m. Satisfying a want of nature; stool, motion; excrement, human ordure; — coming out or forth to battle; fighting.
v.n. To be ruined, lost, &c. = bar-bād honā, q.v.
subs: v.t. To give to the winds, cast or throw away, lose, waste, squander, dissipate, misapply; to lay waste, ruin, destroy, ravage, plunder, &c. — bar-bād honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be ruined, lost, destroyed, thrown away, wasted, squandered, dissipated, laid waste, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برباد (bar-bād, literally “to the wind”). Compare Punjabi بَرْبَاد (barbād), Bengali বরবাদ (borbad).

def: s.f. Loss, ruin, destruction, waste, laying waste, ravage, &c. (syn. ujāṛ; nāś), destruction of crops (in the passage of troops, &c.).
s.f. (?), (local), North wind.
s.m. = baṛwānal, q.v.s.v. baṛwā.
s.f. Muttering, murmuring, grumbling, chattering; talking (in sleep), raving (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To mutter, &c. = baṛbaṛānā, and baṛṛānā.

def: s.f. Muttering, grumbling; chatter, gibberish; light-headed talk; talking incoherently in sleep, raving (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To mutter, to talk gibberish, to blabber, to rave.

def: s.f. Muttering, grumbling; chatter, gibberish; light-headed talk; talking incoherently in sleep, raving (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To mutter, to talk gibberish, to blabber, to rave.

def: v.n. To mutter, murmur, grumble; to chatter, prate, jabber, talk nonsense; to talk in sleep, &c. = baṛṛānā, q.v.
s.f. = baṛbaṛāhaṭ, q.v.
s.f. A large kind of goat (from Barbary).
s.m. Mutterer, grumbler, chatterer, raver.
adj. See s.v. bar, 'a boon, &c.'

ety: S. वर, or वृद्ध = H. बढ़+वश
def: s.m. Power, might, force, strength, prowess, bravery, vigour, violence.
s.f. A species of snake.
s.m. A kind of bean, Dolichos catjang ( = borā).
s.f. A certain musical instrument, the Persian lute (so called from its resemblance to the breast of a duck or goose).
— See s.v. بڙ for this and all compounds beginning with the contrac. adj. baṛ, 'great.'
s.m. Trick, deceit, deception; affectation, hypocrisy (syn. bhagal: cf. next).
adj. & s.m. Deceptive, false, hypocritical; affected; — impostor, trickster, cheat, hypocrite; affected person (syn. bhagliyā: cf. next).
s.m. = barćhā, q.v.
s.m. Small door, postern; verandah, portico; sun-shade.
s.m. A small spear ( = barćhī, q.v.).
s.m. Spear, lance (see barćhī)
subs: (or barćhe-) bardār, s.m. Spear, man, lancer ( = barćhait).

def: s.m. Spearman, lancer.
s.f. Small spear; spear, lance, javelin, dart
s.f. = barćhī, q.v.
s.m. Cold, coldness, frigidity
subs: s.m. Coldness of the extremities, coldness of death.

def: s.m. Prisoner of war, captive, slave
subs: s.m. Slave-merchant, slave-dealer; — barda-faroshī, s.f. Selling (one) as a slave; sale of slaves, slave-trade.

def: s.m. = H برڐي बरडी barḍī, s.f. A light, sandy, or gravelly soil.
s.f. See s.v. bar, 'boon.'
adj. = bardhmān, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. bar 'boon.'
adj. See s.v. bar, 'bosom.'

ety: act. part. of bar-dāshtan; and = Pehl. burtār: Zend beretar, rt. bar; S. bhartar, rt. bhar
def: adj. & s.m. Holding or raising up; bearer, supporter, carrier, taker up (used in comp. e.g. ḥuqqā-bardār, s.m. The servant who looks after and brings in the ḥuqqā
s.f. The act of bearing, carrying, &c.; means of carriage or conveyance (used in comp. e.g. bār-bardārī; ḥuqqā-bardārī).
s.f. Endurance, patience, forbearance; stores, supplies; taking goods on credit, credit transaction (syn. ućāpat
subs: (-kī), To endure, suffer, tolerate, bear patiently (syn. sahnā); to look well (after), tend (an animal) well
v.n. To be endured, &c.; to be tolerable.

def: part. Raised up, elevated; taken up or away, removed.
s.m. Bullock, &c. ( = barad, q.v.).
adj. Increasing, growing; thriving, prosperous; — s.m. A district and city (now called Bardwān).
v.t. To have a cow bulled.
s.f. Drove of laden cattle.
s.f. Papyrus.
s.f. = H برڐا बरडा barḍā, s.m. A light, sandy, or gravelly soil.
part. Sewed; fixed or nailed upon or to (see doḵẖta).
conj. (local), For, for the sake (of), &c. = liye, q.v.
adv. Once, one time, all at once; at last, at length.
adv. At last, at length; once; however.
s.m. = bareṭhā, q.v.
s.m. Ground enclosed for the cultivation of the Piper Betel, betel-garden.
s.m. Ground enclosed for the cultivation of the Piper Betel, betel-garden.
s.m. Ground enclosed for the cultivation of the Piper Betel, betel-garden.
s.m. Beam, rafter.
s.m. Wasp ( = barr, baṛ).
s.m. (Maṛwārī), Venerable old man, sage.
s.m. Silk, &c. = abresham, q.v.
s.f. Negotiating a marriage.
s.f. (?), Name of a tribe of inferior Rājpūts.
s.m. Washerman (syn. dhobī); fuller; land of the third quality; a plot of ground on which sugar-cane has been lately grown.
s.f. Washerwoman, laundress washerman's wife (syn. dhoban).

ety: Pehl. vafr; Zend vafra, rt. vap, 'to throw'
def: s.m. (f. ?), Ice, snow
subs: adj. & s.m. Cooled in ice, iced; — a sherbet, &c. cooled in ice
v.n. To freeze; snow to fall, to snow: barf-sā, adj. (f. -ī), Like ice, cold as ice
(-ko), To put in ice, cool in ice, to ice.

def: adj. Icy; snowy
adj. & s.f. Icy; snowy; iced, cooled; — a kind of sweetmeat made of sugar and milk (having the appearance of ice).
s.m. Leaf (syn. pattā); — warlike apparatus; provisions or necessaries for a journey or march; — a musical instrument; melody
subs: s.m. Leaf of a rose; (met.) lip of a mistress.

def: s.m. Class or multitude of similar things, division, group, class, kind, species, order; section, chapter, division (of a book); class or series of consouants of the alphabet articulated by the same organ (as the cerebrals, dentals, palatals, &c.); (in Alg. and Arith.) square of a number; — adj. Square; — bargātmak (S. varga+ātmaka), adj. Square (as opp. to 'cubic')
subs: s.m. An operation relating to square numbers

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of the same class or kind; like, equal (used by Hindū women)
s.m. The Banyan or Indian fig-tree ( = baṛ).
v.t. = wargalānnā, q.v.
s.m. Division, partition, share, portion; proportion; contribution.
part. Turned back or away; turned, changed; turned over, inverted; revolted, rebelled, apostatized; crossed, unfortunate; unruly, disloyal, refractory, rebellious
subs: To turn over, be inverted; to turn away or aside, revolt, &c.

def: s.f. Turning away from, turning back, turning over; inversion; retrocession; revolt, rebellion, disloyalty, apostasy, abjuration.
s.m. = baṛ and bargat, q.q.v.
s.m. The Banyan or Indian fig-tree ( = baṛ).
s.m. The Ortolan, Alauda.
s.m. See s.v. bar, 'boon.'
s.m. An epithet applied to the Marhaṭṭas; — an uninvited guest (syn. t̤ufail).
s.f. Time, turn (occurring only in comp., e.g. ek bārgī, adv. All at once).
s.m. A trooper who is mounted by the state or the chief he serves, a trooper who does not find his own horse; — a groom, syce ( = bālgīr).
s.m. Name of one of the thirty-six royal castes of Rājpūts.
part. Chosen, selected; elect.
s.f. = bāṛ, 'edge,' &c., and bāṛ, 'volley,' q.q.v.
s.f. Increase, rise; growth, vegetation; promotion; swelling or rise (of a ri er), inundation, flood.
s.m. Tail-feather, plumage of the tail (especially a peacock's tail).
adj. = baṛ, baṛā, q.q.v.
s.m. Rope, string; thong.
s.m. Watercourse; aqueduct; channel for the passage of water from a well to a field, or from one field to another.
s.m. Land of a township which is furthest from the homestead; field where cows feed; field.
adj. = baṛā, q.v
adv. Times out of number, time after time, again and again, repeatedly, often ( = bār-bār).
s.f. = barāhī, q.v.
s.m. = baṛhaʼī, q.v.
s.f. Increase, advancement, &c. ( = baṛhāʼo, q.v.).
s.m. Carpenter; the carpenter caste; — the woodpecker (syn. khuṭ-baṛhaʼī).
s.f. Wife of a carpenter (baṛhaʼī); a woman of the carpenter caste.
s.m. A species of pulse; a grain-measure of one ser (or, in some places, of a ser and a half).
adj. Based upon right; by or of right, rightful, right, true, justifiable, proper; in truth, for or of a certainty; real, actual; positive; incumbent, inevitable.
s.m. The tree Artocarpus lakoocha, and the fruit thereof; — the broad-leaved Sebesten, Cordia latifolia, and its fruit.
adj. Confused, jumbled together, turned upside down or topsy-turvy, entangled, spoiled; offended, angry, vexed, enraged, sullen
subs: s.m. Confusing, mixing together; striking together
s.f. Confusing; embroiling; interference, prevention, putting a stop to
v.t. To throw into confusion or disorder; to embroil, complicate; to mar, ruin, destroy.

def: s.f. Confusion, convulsion, trouble, anarchy; vexation, displeasure, anger, wrath.
s.f. = baṛhaʼin, q.v.

ety: baṛhā˚ = baṛhāw˚ = Prk. वड्ढावे(इ) or वड्ढाव(इ) = S. वर्ध + आपय(ति), rt. वृध् with caus. aug. आपि
def: v.t. To increase, enlarge, extend, lengthen, produce, prolong, stretch, draw out; to advance, promote, raise, exalt, aggrandize; to amplify, magnify, exaggerate; to move forward, move on, carry forward, move away, remove (as a table-cloth), close (a shop, &c.), stop (a child's milk, e.g. dūdh baṛhānā, 'to wean'); to postpone, delay; to extinguish (a lamp)
subs: v.t. To bring forward, lead on (an army, &c.).

def: s.f. = baṛhtī and baṛhotrī, q.q.v.
s.f. = baṛhtī and baṛhotrī, q.q.v.
s.m. Increase, augmentation, enlargement, extension, prolongation; advancement, promotion, exaltation, aggrandizement; rise, swell, inundation; excess, exaggeration; surplus, superfluity
s.m. A Hindū ceremony, feast after marriage.
s.m. = baṛhal, q.v.
s.m. Inducement to move on or forward, encouragement, incitement, incentive, stimulus, spur, excitement; flattery
subs: (-ko), To encourage, incite, stimulate, spur, excite; to flatter.

def: s.m. = baṛhāʼo, q.v.; — s.f. Cake of cow-dung placed on the top of a heap of corn as a charm against the effects of an evil eye.
s.m. A kind of stone = baṛhiyā, q.v.
s.m. = بڙهيلا baṛhelā, q.v.
s.m. A wild hog ( = banelā).
s.m. Name of one of the subdivisions of the ćamār caste; a member of that caste.
s.f. Usurious profit or interest (taken on grain, &c.); interest in kind paid on seed-grain.
s.m. Peacock.
s.f. = baṛhotrī; and s.m. = baṛhaʼī q.q.v.
s.f. Usurious profit or interest (taken on grain, &c.); interest in kind paid on seed-grain.
adj. Superior, excellent, of good quality; rich, productive, fertile (soil); costly, high-priced, dear.
s.m. Name of a kind of sugar millstone extracted from the quarries of Chunār ( = baṛheʼā); — name of a small clan of Rājpūts; a species of pulse, &c. = baṛhaiyā, q.v.
s.m. Name of a disease affecting sugar-cane, Indian corn, &c., which prevents the head from shooting.
s.m. = bāṛiyā, q.v.
s.m. = baṛhāʼī; adj. = baṛhiyā and baṛhyal, q.q.v.
adj. = baṛhiyā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = baṛkā, q.v.
adj. & adv. Greater (than, -se), more, exceeding, surpassing; in a greater degree.
v.t. To fence, &c. = bāṛh (or bāṛ) bāṅdhnā, q.v.
v.n. To increase, &c. = baṛhnā, q.v.

ety: baṛh˚ = Prk. वड्ढ(इ) = S. वर्द्ध(त), rt. वृध्
def: v.n. To increase, enlarge, be enhanced, be extended, be produced, be prolonged; to extend, lengthen; to grow, rise; to swell (as a stream); to be raised, be elevated, be exalted, be promoted; to prosper, flourish, thrive, become rich or powerful, acquire strength or dignity; to gain, acquire (as profit); to go on, proceed, advance, make head; to get beyond, surpass, exceed (with abl.); to be set or put aside; to be cut off, be stopped (as a supply, &c.; e.g. dūdh baṛh-jānā, 'to be weaned'), to be removed or taken away (as a table-cloth); to be closed (as a shop); to be extinguished (as a lamp); to be lowered, to fall (as a rate or price)
subs: v.n. Intens. of, and = baṛhnā, q.v.
baṛh-ke ćalnā, v.n. To be arrogant, proud, &c. = itrānā, q.v.

def: s.f. Increase, growth, prosperity, advancement, rise; advance (paid for manufacture, cultivation, or contract); — broom, brush.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts near Benares.
Increase, addition, increment; advancement, promotion, improvement, advantage, interest, gain, profit; overplus, surplus and balance.
Increase, addition, increment; advancement, promotion, improvement, advantage, interest, gain, profit; overplus, surplus and balance.

adj. (f. -ī), Increasing, advancing, extending, growing, rising, thriving, prosperous; progressing, progressive; accelerated (as motion; e.g. baṛhtī ćāl, 'accelerated motion, or velocity'); additional, supplemental, extra, in excess.
Increase, augmentation, enhancement, advancement, progress, promotion, elevation, exaltation, prosperity, aggrandizement; excess, overplus.
Increase, augmentation, enhancement, advancement, progress, promotion, elevation, exaltation, prosperity, aggrandizement; excess, overplus.
s.m. = H بڙهواري बढ़वारी baṛhwārī, s.f. Increase, prosperity, &c. = baṛhāʼī, q.v.
s.f. = H بڙهوار बढ़वार baṛhwār, s.m. Increase, prosperity, &c. = baṛhāʼī, q.v.
s.f. = bāṛī, q.v.
s.f. A small ear-ring, &c. = bālī, q.v.
s.m. (f. bārin), s.m. A caste of Hindūs who sew together broad leaves to serve as platters; a seller of pān or betel-leaf ( = baraʼī, q.v.).
s.m. The Creator, the Deity
adj. = balī; and s.f. = baṛī, q.q.v.
s.f. Quicklime (syn. ćūnā; kalī).
adj. Free (from, -se), clear, quit (of); irresponsible (for), in a state of immunity (with respect to), exempt (from); acquitted, absolved, discharged, released; innocent, guiltless
subs: adj. Free from charge or obligation; justified; irresponsible, unaccountable
v.t. To free (from, -se), acquit, discharge from liability, relieve from responsibility; to release, exculpate, exonerate
v.n. To be free (from), be acquitted, &c.

def: s.f. Small lumps of pulse (urd, mūng, &c.), fried in oil or ghī, then dried in the sun, and used as condiments, or seasoning of native dishes (cf. baṛā; syn. adaurī); small balls of charcoal used for burning on a ḥuqqā (syn. gul).
s.f. Time, turn; turn for duty; a mode of keeping up the village watch; — a small door, wicket; window
subs: adv. In turn; turn about, each in due course
s.m. (f. bārī-dārnī), An attendant who waits in turn with others

def: s.f. Wedding garment, ornaments, presents, &c. sent from the bridegroom's house to the bride's previous to the wedding day.
s.f. Enclosed piece of ground, garden, orchard, kitchen-garden. plantation, field; cotton-field; house with surrounding garden, &c.; homestead; home, dwelling
subs: bāṛī bīnnā, To pluck the fruit of an orchard, gather the vegetables in a garden or field; to gather the cotton crop

ety: from bārnā = bālnā, q.v.; fem. bārin
def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs whose occupation it is to sell torches; a torchbearer by profession. (The same caste occasionally act as barbers.)
adj. & s.m. = vāri-ćar, q.v.
s.m. Cloud ( = bādal).
adj. Cold, frigid; congealed; firm, solid, established.
adj. Lotus-eyed.
adj. Fine, thin, slender, slight, slim, delicate; minute, nice, subtle; shrill, high (note, &c.)
subs: s.f. Fine or shrill sound; high note; the treble (in music)
s.f. Nice point, subtle question; fine discrimination
adj. & s.m. Penetrating, discriminating, possessed of fine perception, nice, quick of apprehension, intelligent; — critic; judge; connoisseur
s.m. Fine or delicate work; delicate matter or affair, important business

def: s.f. Fineness, minuteness, niceness, subtlety; nicety, delicacy; nice perception, penetration, discernment
subs: (-kī), To discriminate; to make nice or subtle distinctions; to criticize.

def: adj. High, sublime; on high.
s.m. Year = baras, q.v.

ety: abst. S. fr. bārīdan, 'to rain,' see bārān
def: s.f. Rain ( = meṅh; — cf. S. varshā).
s.m. Plantain; seed of fleawort.
adj. Corr. of bal-want, q.v.
adj. Corr. of bal-want, q.v.
s.m. = baraʼī, q.v.
s.f. Betel plantation; garden.
s.m. A whetter (of swords, &c.), knife-grinder.
